asset_name: BPC-157 Human Knee Pain Evidence Lead
status: HUMAN PUBLICATION IDENTIFIED — PRIMARY FULL-TEXT CLOSURE INCOMPLETE — SECONDARY OUTCOME DISCREPANCY LOGGED

publication.title: Intra-Articular Injection of BPC 157 for Multiple Types of Knee Pain
publication.journal: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine

known_human_scope.population_character: Patients with chronic / multiple forms of knee pain

secondary_source_discrepancy.reported_summary: 7 of 12 patients reportedly experienced relief lasting more than six months
11 of 12 subjects reportedly experienced improvement; source also notes lack of validated survey tools

certiaura_interpretation: The discrepancy between current secondary summaries prevents promotion of either shorthand figure as the definitive primary-study result. The study remains uncontrolled / low-certainty human evidence until the primary article is directly extracted.

risk_of_bias_status.validated_outcome_instrument: Secondary reporting raises concern that validated survey tools were not used

permitted_claim: A very small human knee-pain report has been published; it does not establish confirmatory efficacy.
not_permitted: BPC-157 is proven to treat knee pain
BPC-157 is proven to repair knee tissue in humans
The study establishes a universal intra-articular dose
